title: dicentra formosa subsp. oregona
intro: Dicentra formosa subsp. oregona, also known as the western bleeding heart, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Dicentra formosa subsp. oregona can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses.
